references.text: Backer, CA. 1951. Aizoaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 4: 267-275

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 8 January 2026

species.citation: (L.) L. , Syst. Nat., 10th Ed. 2 (1759) 1058 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 88; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 136; Backer, Fl. Males ser. 1, 4 (1951) 272; Cortez et al., Check List 19 (2023) 986; ≡ Portulaca portulacastrum L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 446.
species.distribution: Africa, America, Australia, Bangladesh, Cambodia, Caribbean, China, India, Indian Ocean, Japan, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madagascar, Malay Peninsula, Middle East, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. CEBU (photos), HUNDRED ISLS (photos), KALANGGAMAN, LUZON: Batangas (photos), Bulacan, NCR, Pampanga (photos), Pangasinan (photos), MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, Misamis Oriental (photos), MINDORO, OLANGO, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos), PAPAHAG, SAMAR, SIARGAO, SIASI, SIQUIJOR (photos).
species.notes: In saline, clayey or sandy, humid or muddy sites near the sea and along tidal creeks, often in dense patches. 0-50m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 20 October 2020

species.citation: (Pall.) Kuntze , Revis. Gen. Pl. 1 (1891) 264 ; Backer, Fl. Males ser. 1, 4 (1951) 275; ≡ Demidovia tetragonoides Pall. , Enum. Hort. Demidof (1781) 150.
species.distribution: Australia, Japan, New Zealand, Pacific Ocean.
species.notes: Cultivated mostly 1000-1700m in Malesia. New Zealand spinach.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 16 January 2025

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 223 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 198; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 136; Backer, Fl. Males ser. 1, 4 (1951) 274.
species.distribution: Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines. LUZON: Abra, Albay, Isabela, Laguna, NCR, Pampanga (photos), Rizal, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur (photos), MINDORO, PABELLON ISLS, PALAWAN, PANAY.

species.citation: Willd. ex Spreng. , Syst. Veg., 16th Ed. 2 (1825) 381 ; Willd., Neue Schr. Naturfr. Berlin 4 (1803) 181; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 273.
species.distribution: Africa, Australia, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Philippines. MINDANAO.
